evals.prompt: We've finished the analysis session for GlobalBank. I have s1-01, s1-03 and s1-04 ready and want to design the target AIOps platform. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: Session opening for Phase A: confirms the Session 1 inputs, shows the four-phase architecture structure (constraints, UC selection, platform design, release/governance plan), and asks Phase 1 constraint questions. Does NOT jump to tooling or platform recommendations before UC selection.

assertions.name: phase-a-session-header-present
assertions.description: Response contains the AIOps Architecture Session Phase A opening header (e.g. 'AIOps Architecture — Session 2 of 3' or equivalent)

assertions.name: four-phase-structure-shown
assertions.description: Response includes a table or list showing the four phases (constraints, UC selection, platform design, release/governance plan) and their output files (s2-01 through s2-04)

assertions.name: confirms-session-1-inputs
assertions.description: Response references the required Session 1 inputs (s1-01 context, s1-03 findings, s1-04 UC catalogue)

assertions.name: asks-constraints-first
assertions.description: Response asks Phase 1 constraint questions (cloud preference, data residency, team model, build-vs-buy) before designing the platform

assertions.name: no-premature-tooling
assertions.description: Response does NOT recommend specific tools, platforms, or architecture components before UC selection and constraint gathering

evals.prompt: The architecture session for Meridian Logistics is done. I have my four session 2 files ready: s2-01 through s2-04. Time to generate the architecture report.
evals.expected_output: Phase B session opening: asks about report format, client name, logo, date, confidentiality. Does NOT re-run Phase A or ask for Session 1 analysis files.

assertions.name: phase-b-correctly-identified
assertions.description: Response correctly identifies this as Phase B (report generation) rather than Phase A (interactive architecture)

assertions.name: asks-report-format
assertions.description: Response asks about report format options — at minimum mentions Accenture HTML and at least one alternative

assertions.name: asks-client-and-date
assertions.description: Response asks for client name (exact spelling/case) and document date

assertions.name: no-phase-a-content
assertions.description: Response does NOT attempt to restart the architecture design or ask for Session 1 analysis inputs to redo discovery

assertions.name: mentions-input-files
assertions.description: Response references or lists the four s2 input files needed (s2-01 through s2-04), optionally noting s1-03 for F-## traceability

evals.prompt: We have the analysis findings and use-case catalogue for a retail banking client and need to figure out what platform to build and how to roll it out. How should I run this?
evals.expected_output: Correctly infers Phase A from the signals (has findings + UC catalogue, wants platform design). Opens with the structured four-phase architecture protocol and treats the reference architecture as a menu, not a template.

assertions.name: infers-phase-a-from-context
assertions.description: Response correctly infers this is Phase A (the user has Session 1 outputs and wants to design) without asking the user to clarify Phase A vs Phase B

assertions.name: session-structure-shown
assertions.description: Response presents the structured four-phase session layout with output files

assertions.name: grounds-in-findings
assertions.description: Response ties the design work to the Session 1 findings (F-##) and selected use cases rather than designing in the abstract

assertions.name: reference-architecture-as-menu
assertions.description: Response treats the reference architecture pattern library as a menu of options to select from, not a fixed template to copy wholesale
